'''Dragon's Spine''' was an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Although it served as a center point of navigation for local [[Scout/Legends|explorers]], stories of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] colliding with [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] were not unheard of. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Dragon's Spine hosted a conflict between the thug factions of [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]] and [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]].

==Description==
The massive Dragon's Spine was one of the oldest [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid fields]] in the vicinity of the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo]] [[Star system/Legends|star system]]. Located right outside [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]],<ref name="SWGCG" /> a major [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the system,<ref name="EAWGG">[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> it snaked across the vast expanse of space around the planet and was generally safe enough to serve as a center point of navigation for many explorers.<ref name="SWGCG" />

==History==
{{Quote|Eh, so you had a bit of a rough landing, I think. Too bad for you, eh? New ships don't come cheap.|[[Watto/Legends|Watto]] to the marooned spacer|<ref name="JGG">''[[Star Wars Math: Jabba's Game Galaxy]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Tatooine_asteroid_field.png|thumb|left|250px|The Dragon's Spine asteroids in the waning days of the Galactic Republic]]
At some point before the [[Boonta Eve Classic (32 BBY)/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Timeline">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' states that the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] took place in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], and Ratts Tyerell died during the film. As Tyerell was shown to be alive in ''Jabba's Game Galaxy'', it must have taken place before the events of ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> an [[Unidentified extra-galactic spacer|unidentified spacer]] from another [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] collided with an asteroid while flying through the field, which caused their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] to enter a spin and crash on Tatooine, in the vicinity of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]].<ref name="JGG" />

Much to the consternation of the local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]], his [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] competitor [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Valarian/Legends|Valarian]] maintained a military presence in the Dragon's Spine during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. At the time, even the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had ordered their [[Imperial Navy/Legends|fleets]] to stay away from the Spine and the conflict that raged within the asteroid field.<ref name="SWGCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
There are only a minority of sources that show an asteroid field right outside Tatooine, as none can been seen in the six ''Star Wars'' movies. The first of these sources was the [[1993]] video game ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]''. The field also appeared in ''[[Star Wars Math: Jabba's Game Galaxy]]'', a educational video game released in [[1999]], and in the now defunct ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', in which it was first identified as the Dragon's Spine.
